response:
Short answer: by pulling the raw Service Bus operational blobs from the storage account where SB logs were being forwarded (not Log Analytics), searching for the failing request TrackingId, and matching that blob entry to the operational log entry for the same timestamp — which revealed the failed "Retrieve Subscription" (GetSubscription) call and the client pattern/likely source app.

What we actually did (step‑by‑step)
- Noticed 40100 (unauthorized) GetSubscription errors in the prod-azure-v3 namespace.
- Checked where Service Bus operational logs were landing and discovered they were only being forwarded to Datadog storage (not Log Analytics).
- Opened the raw blob logs in the storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70 and searched for the request payload/TrackingId.
- Found the exact payload with TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17.
- Matched that blob entry to the insights-logs-operationallogs record for 2026-04-15 hour 16 (EventTimeString 4/15/2026 4:48:51 PM +00:00): EventName "Retrieve Subscription", Status "Failed".
- From the request details we inferred the client pattern and therefore the likely source app responsible for the unauthorized calls.

Key artifact: TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 and the failed "Retrieve Subscription" operational log entry at 2026-04-15T16:48:51Z in the storage blobs.
context_injected: {"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "debug", "facts": ["APIM authentication debugging pattern → targets → APIM", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → uses → APIM policy XML", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → used_by → auth debugging sessions", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → integrates_with → agent-fleet-api", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → used_by → ApiManagementGatewayLogs", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → uses → APIM on-error policy", "ServiceNow → uses → Northwind Traders"], "recent": ["2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, traced Service Bus 40100 unauthorized GetSubscription errors in namespace prod-azure-v3 to a specific client pattern and likely source app. Service Bus operational logs were only configured to Datadog forwarding storage, not Log Analytics, so the raw blob logs in storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70 had to be searched. The exact payload with TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 was found in insights-logs-operationallogs for 2026-04-15 hour 16. Matching record: EventTimeString 4/15/2026 4:48:51 PM +00:00, EventName 'Retrieve Subscription', Status 'Failed', Call", "2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. T", "2026-04-03: On 2026-04-03 in fleet-platform-app prod telemetry, investigated two incidents during a 60-minute deployment watch. Main app incident: between 17:33:55Z and 17:34:20Z, send_bulk_operation_callback failed with RetryableHTTPError HTTP 500 after 4 attempts from BULK_AUTOMATION_CALLBACK_URL=https://governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback. Multiple distinct returned correlationIds were observed (including 9e3e8b0f-43ce-4c05-8885-1c8b48dc88be and 7c189fbd-4a41-45b1-af1e-90835f0874c2). This maps to policy_rule_events.py callback send path and bulk_callback_client/ht", "2026-04-01: April 1, 2026 prod App Insights triage for fleet platform: main app azr-fleet-platform-prod showed no active exceptions or failed requests in the queried 24h window for resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger after prior fixes. Remaining active failures were in azr-fleet-webhook-prod: 4 recent WebhookEnqueueFailure 503s caused by transient Microsoft.ServiceBus send timeouts during sender.send_messages. Patched functions-webhook/function_app.py to retry once on transient send timeout using a fresh retired transport/client while keeping the same overall operation timeout budget, and added regression t", "2026-03-20: On 2026-03-20, live Azure investigation of Log Analytics workspace DefaultWorkspace-5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661-SCUS in subscription 5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661 (Azure Engineering - Production Subscription) found AppExceptions billed usage of 1552.998 GB over 90 days. 95.5% of this was from App Insights component prod-azure-audit-engine / Function App prod-azure-audit-engine in resource group ussc-prod-audit-engine-rg001. Dominant signature: Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusException at AmqpConnectionScope.CreateReceivingLinkAsync with 101,940,682 rows and 1483.83 GB from", "2026-03-20: Webhook incident on March 20, 2026: prod webhook ingress was healthy and Service Bus auth worked, but main function apps in dev/prod were missing the new resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger live even though code/app settings had been updated. Root cause was trigger metadata not synced for the main app after adding a new Service Bus trigger; manual POST to Microsoft.Web/sites/<app>/syncfunctiontriggers immediately surfaced resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger in both dev and prod. After sync, resource-change-ingest messages drained but failed with ValueError 'Invalid resource-change ingest message: ", "2026-03-20: On 2026-03-20 during prod health validation of fleet-platform-app after PR #59 deployment, azr-fleet-platform-prod main app was healthy but azr-fleet-webhook-prod was in an active outage. Public and direct webhook health endpoints returned 500, while direct main /api/health returned 200. App Insights over the prior 30 minutes showed about 71,770 webhook requests with about 70,524 failures. Dominant exceptions were System.InvalidOperationException 'Did not find any initialized language workers' and FunctionTimeoutException at 00:00:30 for Functions.eventgrid_resource_change_webhook. 5-minute bi", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, read-only inspection of the prod Service Bus topic subscription azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall correlated visible topic messages to governance callback 404s in azr-compliance-v3. Catchall active backlog had 3697 messages and 1223 dead-letter messages; dead-letter items were only TTLExpiredException from 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-05, unrelated to the incident. Incident-hour correlation: 2026-03-17T02:00Z had 4 catchall messages and 4 callback 404s; all 4 were policy-label AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_CREATE_REQUESTED with username FAzureProdEngAutomation. 2026-03-17T15:00Z ha", "2026-03-19: For the 2026-03-19 azr-compliance-v3 prod callback 404 investigation, the python-httpx client was traced to the external Fleet Platform prod function app, not the compliance repo backend. Evidence: the shared ASE outbound IP 172.206.172.169 is used by multiple apps, but azr-fleet-platform-prod and azr-fleet-webhook-prod are the only prod Fleet Python function apps. The automation Service Bus topic in namespace azr-fleet-platform-events has semantic subscriptions policy/alerts/ama/tags; the policy subscription is filtered on label=policy, matching the compliance app's documented bulk automation"]}
